tests.name: init_sh_exits_zero
tests.details: init.sh exits 1 when push to fake remote (https://example.com/fake.git) fails. All artifacts created prior; failure is at create_and_protect_remote -> host=other path: 'Push failed — verify URL and credentials'. Expected per runbook: framework should support a fake-remote/no-push UAT mode.

framework_bugs.summary: init.sh has no scriptable mode for UAT (interactive only)
framework_bugs.reproduction: bash init.sh <<EOF ... EOF — heredoc must perfectly match conditional prompt order; any environment-driven branch (Docker install, tool walkthrough, etc.) breaks the driver. Only --dry-run and --help flags exist.
framework_bugs.expected: A --non-interactive / --config FILE mode that accepts a JSON or kv answer file so UAT, CI smoke tests, and re-installs are deterministic.
framework_bugs.actual: Heredoc-only; verified working today against this specific environment but every prompt (even visibility 1/2) requires correct positional answers.

framework_bugs.summary: init.sh aborts with exit 1 when fake remote push fails, leaving project half-initialized
framework_bugs.reproduction: Run init.sh, choose host=other, paste a non-resolvable URL (e.g., https://example.com/fake.git). create_and_protect_remote prints '[FAIL] Push failed — verify URL and credentials' and returns 1; main() does not finalize verify-install or print_next_steps, but all earlier artifacts are persisted.
framework_bugs.expected: Either (a) a documented --skip-remote / --offline flag for evaluation flows, or (b) a recovery prompt offering to retry with a different URL or skip the push step.
framework_bugs.actual: Hard fail with no recovery path; manifest.json + process-state.json never get the host/mode/remote_url fields populated, leaving phase2_init.remote_repo_created falsely true (per --verify-init) but branch_protection_configured false.

documentation_gaps.gap: phase-state.json schema is missing the phase_2_to_3 gate slot — only phase_0_to_1, phase_1_to_2, and phase_3_to_4 are pre-allocated. check-phase-gate.sh reads phase_2_to_3 but the init template never creates the key.

Runbook §A 'init driver' implies a scriptable mode might exist; it doesn't. The fallback heredoc strategy is the only option, but the prompt sequence shifts based on environment (Docker present? Qdrant registered? CDF cloned?). A reference 'canonical answer file' alongside the runbook would let agents reproduce results.

tests.name: bl006_blocks_feat_without_loop
tests.details: PreToolUse path: piped {"command":"git commit ... feat(bl006-trigger): no loop"} into pre-commit-gate.sh — denied with permissionDecision=deny when build_loop.feature=null. Note: when build_loop is in a 'completed but not reset' state (feature_recorded done, loop never cleared), BL-006 falls through and allows new feat: commits. See gap below.

tests.name: bl015_sentinel_resolves
tests.details: scripts/pending-approval.sh --resolve removed sentinel; subsequent commit attempt allowed (exit 0 from gate)

tests.name: recorded_feature_appears_in_progress
tests.details: .claude/build-progress.json features_completed = ["uat-feat-1"]

framework_bugs.summary: BL-006 does not reset build_loop after feature_recorded; second feat: commit slips through
framework_bugs.reproduction: Complete a build loop end-to-end (--start-feature 'foo'; complete all 6 steps including feature_recorded). Without --start-feature for a new feature, write a new feat: commit. process-checklist.sh --check-commit-message returns exit 0 because build_loop.feature is still 'foo' and steps 1–5 are still marked complete.
framework_bugs.expected: After feature_recorded, the build_loop should reset (feature=null, steps_completed=[]) so the very next feat: commit re-triggers the BL-006 gate.
framework_bugs.actual: Stale state allows an unbounded number of subsequent feat: commits to bypass BL-006 until build_loop is manually reset.

documentation_gaps.gap: Runbook §C says 'attempt commit; pre-commit-gate.sh blocks via PreToolUse hook' but the gate is only registered as a Claude Code PreToolUse hook — direct shell `git commit` bypasses it. The git pre-commit hook (.git/hooks/pre-commit) covers gitleaks/Semgrep/TDD ordering but does not call the BL-006 build-loop check. Worth clarifying whether agent-only enforcement is by design.

documentation_gaps.gap: lint-uat-scenarios.sh requires a single positional argument and prints only 'Usage:' if called bare — runbook tells agents to 'run scripts/lint-uat-scenarios.sh against generated UAT scenario' without naming the populated HTML the script expects. A short pointer to templates/uat/test-session-template.html as the canonical input would help.

summary: Phases 0-3 fundamentally work for desktop/typescript/full/sponsored_poc; Phase 4 correctly blocked by POC mode. Two High/Medium framework bugs surfaced (init.sh push hard-fail on fake remote leaves project half-init, BL-006 stale build_loop state allows feat: bypass) plus the recurring init.sh non-scriptability gap. Critical happy paths all green: build loop, BL-015 sentinel, BL-006 fresh-state denial, hook registration, verify-install (52/52 passed), pre-commit gate denial via PreToolUse path.
